description: Autonomous AI agent for the NM i AI competition that interprets natural language accounting prompts and executes Tripletex API calls via an OpenAI LLM

userStories.title: Project scaffolding and configuration
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the Python project structure with dependency management and environment configuration so that I can start building the agent.
acceptanceCriteria: pyproject.toml with dependencies: fastapi, uvicorn, requests, openai, python-dotenv, pymupdf, pydantic, pytest, pytest-asyncio, responses, pytest-cov
.env.example with OPENAI_API_KEY, OPENAI_MODEL (default gpt-4o), PORT (default 8000), API_KEY (optional)
.gitignore includes .env, __pycache__, .pytest_cache, *.pyc, .venv
src/__init__.py, src/config.py, src/main.py exist
config.py loads env vars with python-dotenv and exposes a Settings dataclass with openai_api_key, openai_model, port, api_key fields
main.py creates a FastAPI app with a placeholder root route returning {"status": "ok"}
App starts with: python -m src.main (uvicorn on configured port)
Tests pass

userStories.title: Pydantic models and /solve endpoint skeleton
userStories.description: As the competition platform, I need a POST /solve endpoint that accepts the competition request format and returns {"status": "completed"} so that the agent can receive tasks.
acceptanceCriteria: src/models.py with Pydantic models: FileAttachment (filename, content_base64, mime_type), TripletexCredentials (base_url, session_token), SolveRequest (prompt, files: list[FileAttachment], tripletex_credentials: TripletexCredentials), SolveResponse (status: str)
POST /solve endpoint in main.py accepts SolveRequest body
If API_KEY is set in config, endpoint validates Authorization: Bearer header and returns 401 if invalid
Endpoint currently returns {"status": "completed"} as placeholder
Endpoint catches exceptions and returns HTTP 500 with error detail
Unit tests: valid request returns 200, invalid body returns 422, wrong API key returns 401
Tests pass

userStories.title: Tripletex API client
userStories.description: As the agent, I need an HTTP client to interact with the Tripletex API through the provided proxy URL so I can CRUD entities.
acceptanceCriteria: src/tripletex_client.py with TripletexClient class taking base_url and session_token
Uses requests library with Basic Auth (username '0', password = session_token)
Methods: get(endpoint, params=None), post(endpoint, json=None), put(endpoint, json=None), delete(endpoint)
get() supports fields, count, and from_ parameters for pagination and field selection
All methods return parsed JSON (resp.json())
Logs every API call: method, endpoint, status_code using Python logging
Raises TripletexAPIError(status_code, message) for 4xx/5xx responses, parsing error detail from response body
Unit tests using responses library: test successful GET/POST/PUT/DELETE, test 4xx raises TripletexAPIError, test pagination params are sent correctly
Tests pass

userStories.title: File processing pipeline
userStories.description: As the agent, I need to extract text and data from PDF and image attachments so I can include their content when interpreting tasks.
acceptanceCriteria: src/file_processor.py with FileProcessor class
process_files(files: list[FileAttachment], openai_api_key: str, openai_model: str) -> list[dict] returning [{filename, extracted_text}]
Decodes base64 content from FileAttachment
For PDFs: uses pymupdf (fitz) to extract text from all pages
For PDFs with no extractable text (scanned): falls back to OpenAI vision API by rendering pages as images
For image files (PNG, JPG, JPEG): sends to OpenAI vision API with prompt 'Extract all text and data from this image'
Returns empty list when files list is empty
Unit tests: test base64 decoding, test empty files list, test PDF text extraction with a simple test PDF fixture
Tests pass

userStories.title: Tripletex API knowledge base
userStories.description: As the LLM, I need a comprehensive reference of Tripletex API endpoints and patterns so I generate correct execution plans on the first attempt.
acceptanceCriteria: src/knowledge/__init__.py and src/knowledge/tripletex_api.py exist
tripletex_api.py exports TRIPLETEX_API_REFERENCE as a string constant

Documents these endpoints with method, path, required fields, optional fields, and example payload: /employee (GET, POST, PUT), /customer (GET, POST, PUT), /product (GET, POST), /invoice (GET, POST), /order (GET, POST), /travelExpense (GET, POST, PUT, DELETE), /project (GET, POST), /department (GET, POST), /ledger/account (GET), /ledger/voucher (GET, POST, DELETE)
Documents common task patterns: create single entity, create with linking (customer->order->invoice), modify existing, delete/reverse, enable modules, multi-step (payment registration)
Documents module enablement patterns (e.g., enabling department accounting before creating departments)
Documents common errors: missing prerequisites, duplicate entities, required field omissions, Norwegian character handling (æ, ø, å)
Documents that sandbox starts empty — agent must create all prerequisites from scratch
Documents authentication (Basic Auth, username 0), field selection (?fields=), pagination (?count=&from=), date format (YYYY-MM-DD), response wrapper ({fullResultSize, values})
String fits within ~4000 tokens (suitable for inclusion in LLM system prompt)
Tests pass

userStories.title: LLM plan generation
userStories.description: As the agent, I need to use an OpenAI LLM to interpret the task prompt and generate a structured execution plan of Tripletex API calls.
acceptanceCriteria: src/plan_generator.py with PlanGenerator class taking openai_api_key and model name
Pydantic models in src/models.py: PlanStep (step_number, action: GET/POST/PUT/DELETE, endpoint, payload: dict|None, params: dict|None, description), ExecutionPlan (steps: list[PlanStep])
generate_plan(prompt, file_contents: list[dict]|None, error_context: str|None) -> ExecutionPlan

System prompt includes the TRIPLETEX_API_REFERENCE, instructions to output valid JSON matching ExecutionPlan schema, and efficiency guidelines (minimize calls, reuse IDs from responses, avoid unnecessary GETs)
System prompt instructs the model to handle prompts in any of 7 languages: nb, nn, en, es, pt, de, fr
Uses placeholder syntax $stepN.path.to.value for referencing results from previous steps (e.g., $step1.value.id)
Parses LLM JSON response into ExecutionPlan pydantic model
Unit tests with mocked OpenAI response: test valid plan is parsed, test plan with placeholders
Tests pass

userStories.title: Plan execution engine
userStories.description: As the agent, I need to execute the generated plan step-by-step against the Tripletex API, resolving inter-step dependencies.
acceptanceCriteria: src/plan_executor.py with PlanExecutor class taking a TripletexClient
execute(plan: ExecutionPlan) -> ExecutionResult where ExecutionResult has steps_completed: int, results: list[dict], errors: list[str], success: bool
Executes steps in order using the TripletexClient methods based on step.action
Resolves $stepN.path.to.value placeholders in payloads and params by traversing previous step results using dot-notation
Stores each step result in a context dict keyed by step_number
On step failure (TripletexAPIError): records error message, stops execution, returns partial results with success=False
Unit tests with mocked TripletexClient: test sequential execution, test placeholder resolution (e.g., $step1.value.id resolves to 42), test failure stops execution
Tests pass

userStories.title: Re-planning on failure
userStories.description: As the agent, I need to recover from API errors by asking the LLM for a corrected plan so that transient issues don't cause task failure.
acceptanceCriteria: Add replan method to PlanGenerator: replan(original_prompt, file_contents, execution_result: ExecutionResult, error_context: str) -> ExecutionPlan
replan sends the original prompt, what was accomplished so far (successful steps + their results), and the error details to the LLM
LLM generates a corrected plan for remaining work only (not re-doing successful steps)
Add re-planning loop logic: on execution failure, call replan and re-execute, max 2 re-plan attempts
If all retries exhausted, return final partial ExecutionResult
Unit tests: test replan generates corrected plan, test max 2 retries enforced
Tests pass

userStories.title: Orchestrator wiring everything together
userStories.description: As the /solve endpoint, I need an orchestrator that coordinates file processing, plan generation, execution, and re-planning into a single workflow.
acceptanceCriteria: src/orchestrator.py with TaskOrchestrator class taking config Settings

solve(request: SolveRequest) -> SolveResponse method that: 1) processes files via FileProcessor, 2) generates plan via PlanGenerator, 3) executes plan via PlanExecutor, 4) re-plans on failure (up to 2 retries), 5) returns SolveResponse(status='completed')
Creates TripletexClient from request credentials
Logs the full workflow: prompt received, files processed, plan generated (step count), execution result (steps completed, errors), total duration
Catches all exceptions and still returns {status: completed} (competition requires this)
Update /solve endpoint in main.py to use TaskOrchestrator
Integration test with mocked OpenAI and mocked Tripletex: full flow from request to response
Tests pass

userStories.title: Multi-language prompt test coverage
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to verify the agent handles prompts in all 7 supported languages correctly.
acceptanceCriteria: tests/fixtures/sample_prompts.py with sample 'create employee' prompts in: Norwegian Bokmal, Norwegian Nynorsk, English, Spanish, Portuguese, German, French
Each prompt includes a name with special characters (ae, oe, aa, n-tilde, etc.)
Unit tests verify PlanGenerator produces valid plans for prompts in at least nb, en, and es (using mocked OpenAI)
Test verifies special characters are preserved in generated plan payloads
Tests pass

userStories.title: Health check endpoint and structured logging
userStories.description: As an operator, I need a health check endpoint and structured JSON logging so I can monitor the deployed agent.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /health returns {"status": "ok"} with HTTP 200
src/logging_config.py sets up structured JSON logging with fields: timestamp, level, message, extra context
Logging is initialized in main.py on startup
All existing log calls in tripletex_client, orchestrator use the structured logger
Session tokens and API keys are never logged (redacted or excluded)
Unit test: GET /health returns 200
Tests pass

userStories.title: Efficiency optimization in plan generation
userStories.description: As a competitor, I need the agent to minimize API calls and avoid 4xx errors to earn the efficiency bonus on perfect tasks.
acceptanceCriteria: System prompt in PlanGenerator explicitly instructs: reuse IDs from POST responses (don't re-fetch), never call GET for entities you just created, batch requests where Tripletex supports it, validate required fields before calling
PlanExecutor logs total API call count and error count after execution
Orchestrator logs efficiency summary: total_api_calls, error_count, replan_count per /solve request
Add efficiency tracking fields to ExecutionResult: total_api_calls: int, error_count: int
Tests pass

userStories.title: Dockerfile and Docker Compose
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a Docker setup so I can build, test, and deploy the agent as a container to GCP.
acceptanceCriteria: Dockerfile: Python 3.12-slim base, copies pyproject.toml and installs deps first (layer caching), copies src/, exposes PORT, runs uvicorn with --host 0.0.0.0
docker-compose.yml: builds from Dockerfile, mounts .env, maps port 8000:8000
docker build succeeds without errors
docker-compose up starts the app and GET /health returns 200
Tests pass

userStories.title: Integration tests with mocked APIs
userStories.description: As a developer, I need integration tests simulating the full agent flow with mocked external services so I can verify correctness without hitting real APIs.
acceptanceCriteria: tests/test_integration.py with full-flow integration tests
Mock fixtures for OpenAI API returning realistic ExecutionPlan JSON for: create employee, create customer, create invoice (multi-step)
Mock fixtures for Tripletex API responses matching each plan's expected calls
Test 'create employee': verifies POST /employee is called with correct name/email fields
Test 'create invoice': verifies correct order of calls (POST /customer -> POST /order -> POST /invoice) with ID linking
Test 'error recovery': mocks a 422 on first attempt, verifies replan is called and second attempt succeeds
All mocks use responses library
Tests pass

userStories.title: End-to-end test setup with Tripletex sandbox
userStories.description: As a developer, I need e2e tests against the real Tripletex sandbox API to validate the agent works against the actual API.
acceptanceCriteria: tests/e2e/conftest.py loads sandbox credentials from TRIPLETEX_BASE_URL and TRIPLETEX_SESSION_TOKEN env vars (or .env.test)
pytest marker @pytest.mark.e2e so e2e tests run separately with: pytest -m e2e
tests/e2e/test_create_employee.py: creates an employee via TripletexClient, verifies via GET that employee exists with correct fields
tests/e2e/test_create_customer.py: creates a customer, verifies via GET
tests/e2e/test_full_flow.py: sends a full SolveRequest to the orchestrator with a real prompt and sandbox credentials (requires OPENAI_API_KEY), verifies entity was created
README section documenting how to configure and run e2e tests
Tests pass (unit tests; e2e tests pass when sandbox credentials are provided)
